message:
ft-xbnl0.5.1: rewrite operator docs around final asupersync-native architecture

Documentation polish slice for the ft-xbnl0.5.1 bead ("Rewrite README,
AGENTS, and operator docs around the final native Asupersync
architecture and honest support matrix").  No code changes; this only
aligns narrative and operator docs with the reality of the finished
system.

AGENTS.md
- Reframe the runtime policy section: tokio is still forbidden, but
  runtime_compat is now described as an audited boundary for runtime
  lifecycle, channels, time, and blocking work — not migration debt.
- Replace the "Strategic Direction" bullet list with an explicit
  description of the asupersync-native swarm runtime as the intended
  architecture, plus the current implementation reality (ft-owned
  observability, policy, workflow, search, robot surfaces; WezTerm-
  backed pane interop as an implementation boundary, not identity).
- Link the four finish-line truth documents (verification-contract,
  supported-path truth sweep, 4.6 + 5.7 completion evidence).
- Update the architecture ASCII and components table so the backend
  row reads "Current mux interop boundary (WezTerm-backed today)"
  instead of "Backend Adapters", and runtime_compat is described as
  an audited boundary rather than a quarantine being dismantled.

README.md
- Rewrite "Platform Direction" as "Platform Model" — leads with the
  asupersync-native runtime, lists the current implementation
  reality, and anchors support claims to the same four truth docs.
- Add a new "Supported Surface Matrix" table that spells out status
  per surface (watch/status/triage/doctor, search/events/audit, robot
  mode, session persistence, web API + SSE, distributed mode, semantic
  search, browser auth).  Honest about "supported with backend
  prerequisite" vs "supported with explicit limitations".
- Rework "Limitations" / "Known Limitations" tables to stop talking
  about migration debt as a limitation and instead name concrete
  missing capabilities (backend-free live pane interop, live remote
  get-text in distributed mode, arbitrary GUI automation, green
  release gates across every lane).
- Polish the install / quickstart text so "compatibility backend
  bridge" is replaced by "current mux interop boundary" language.

docs/operator-playbook.md
- Add an "Operator reality check" callout at the top anchoring the
  playbook to the same truth docs (verification contract, 4.6 + 5.7
  completion evidence).
- Add a "First Run / Bootstrap" section with the canonical
  command sequence (ft doctor [--json], ft status --health,
  wezterm cli list, ft watch --foreground, ft robot state) and
  per-command interpretation notes.
- Extend the "Related guides" list with the supported-path truth
  sweep reference.

docs/cli-reference.md
- Add a "command-truth companion" preamble cross-referencing the
  four finish-line truth documents.
- Correct minor command shapes: ft status now takes --health, ft show
  is no longer listed as stub, ft doctor --json is documented, and
  ft reproduce is shown with its real export/replay subcommands.

.beads/issues.jsonl
- Routine beads->JSONL flush capturing the in_progress touch on
  ft-xbnl0.5.1 that accompanies this work.

files.patch:
@@ -111,7 +111,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","title":"Rewrite README, AGENTS, and operator docs around the final native Asupersync architecture and honest support matrix","description":"# Goal
Make the repository narrative match the finished system.

This task exists because docs that still describe migration debt, placeholder capabilities, or outdated runtime assumptions will immediately undermine the credibility of the finish-line push.","design":"# Design
1. Rewrite the high-level architecture story around the final native Asupersync model.
2. Update AGENTS guidance and operator docs to reflect the real supported capability matrix, verification expectations, and live health or diagnostic surfaces added by this program.
3. Remove or rewrite stale references to transitional architecture that no longer exists.
4. Keep the docs specific enough that future agents can work from them without reopening old planning documents.","acceptance_criteria":"1. README, AGENTS, and core operator docs describe the final system rather than a migration in progress.
2. Runtime, support-matrix, and verification language is accurate and current.
3. Operators and future agents can understand the intended architecture and supported surfaces directly from the docs.
4. The updated docs are cross-linked to the finish-line evidence model where appropriate.
5. Documentation examples, support claims, and operator procedures are validated against the shipped commands, recorded verification artifacts, live diagnostic surfaces, and the first-run or recovery path so the docs cannot drift ahead of reality.","notes":"This bead is the documentation counterpart to the code cleanup. If the narrative stays stale, the project will feel unfinished even after the code improves.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.852785Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:45:19.088127Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","docs","goal-line","operator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.535955Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:42.715747Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.022385Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.471912Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:31.852785Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:09.776559Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:14.259339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","title":"Add permanent CI, doctor, and audit guards for zero tokio smol and zero fake supported capabilities","description":"# Goal
Make the finish-line invariants self-defending.

This task exists because the project needs durable protection against two classes of regression: runtime debt creeping back in and fake supported capabilities quietly reappearing.","design":"# Design
1. Add or extend CI checks, doctor surfaces, and audit scripts that defend the no-Tokio no-Smol property and the honest support-matrix property.
2. Keep the checks actionable so failures explain what violated the rule and where.
3. Wire the checks into the normal contributor path instead of leaving them as optional extras.
4. Reuse the evidence and inventories built earlier in the program where possible.
5. Validate not only code invariants but also the verification contract itself where practical, including required artifact manifests, log schema, and deterministic E2E entrypoints.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The key finish-line invariants are defended by permanent automated or semi-automated checks.
2. Contributors can run the relevant checks locally and in CI.
3. Violations are specific enough to fix quickly.
4. The release lane can trust these guards as part of ongoing maintenance.
5. Selected deterministic E2E scripts, artifact manifests, and structured-log expectations are automatically checked or linted so the testing and evidence discipline in this program does not silently decay.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This bead is how the finish-line work stays finished after the first release candidate.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyTiger","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.309029Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:48:43.341734Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T16:48:43.341498Z","close_reason":"finish-line guards passing; artifact docs/ft-xbnl0-5-2-finish-line-guards-validation.json","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","ci","doctor","goal-line","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:05.793483Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:43.811867Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:44.145075Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.309029Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","title":"Define blessed tuning profiles and operator playbooks for 10, 50, and 200 plus pane fleets","description":"# Goal
Turn the performance and reliability findings into concrete operating guidance.

This task exists because a finished swarm terminal should not leave operators guessing which settings, limits, and practices are appropriate for small, medium, and very large fleets.","design":"# Design
1. Derive tuning profiles from the reliability and soak evidence instead of speculation.
2. Document the practical knobs, warning signs, diagnostic surfaces, and recommended operating patterns for 10, 50, and 200 plus pane deployments.
3. Keep the guidance honest about which workloads are supported and which are still aspirational.
4. Package the result so future operators can use it without reading the whole backlog.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The project has concrete tuning guidance for representative small, medium, and large fleet sizes.
2. The guidance is tied to measured evidence rather than vague rules of thumb.
3. Operators can use the playbooks and diagnostic surfaces to configure and monitor the system responsibly.
4. The playbooks are integrated with the final docs lane.
5. The playbooks cite exact verification scripts, thresholds, logged artifacts, and escalation paths instead of vague advice.","notes":"This bead turns performance work into something that operators can actually act on.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.737574Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T19:44:43.844749Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T19:44:43.844353Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete; artifacts docs/ft-xbnl0-5-3-blessed-tuning-validation.json and tests/e2e/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.5.3/blessed_tuning_profiles/20260419T193608Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","operator","performance","tuning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.072933Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:44.582153Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:32.737574Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:10.265504Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3556,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyTiger claiming docs/playbook slice: blessed fleet profiles + operator guidance docs tied to 4.6 gate artifacts; avoiding benchmark/runtime implementation surfaces.","created_at":"2026-04-19T19:34:05Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","title":"Run original-mission operator acceptance scenarios from fresh install through long-haul recovery","description":"# Goal
Prove the original user mission in end-to-end operator terms.

This task exists because the finish line is not a set of isolated unit victories. It is a story that starts from setup, moves through normal multi-agent operation, survives errors and disconnects, and still looks trustworthy after long-haul use.","design":"# Design
1. Define acceptance scenarios that cover clean setup, environment validation, normal swarm operation, pane search and control, failure recovery, restore, incident triage, and long-haul stability checks.
2. Run those scenarios against the system with the final guardrails, setup or recovery polish, and tuning guidance in place.
3. Capture artifacts that make the result auditable by someone who did not watch the run live.
4. Record exactly what still feels rough or unsupported if any scenario falls short.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The project has end-to-end operator acceptance scenarios that directly represent the original mission.
2. Each scenario has stored artifacts and exact run procedures.
3. Success and failure criteria are explicit enough that future release candidates can repeat them.
4. Remaining gaps, if any, are documented honestly rather than hidden in a success narrative.
5. The scenario set explicitly covers clean setup, broken-environment diagnosis, standard incident triage through the new diagnostic surfaces, and recovery entry back to steady-state operation.
6. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the human and operator counterpart to the soak and deterministic reliability lanes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.189038Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:16.119166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["acceptance","e2e","goal-line","operator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.332257Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:08.035141Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:44.892317Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:45.331092Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.189038Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:45.786551Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:46.265550Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:10.762074Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:13.106214Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","title":"Assemble the final closure bundle, remaining-risk register, and release-candidate checklist","description":"# Goal
Package the finish-line work into one auditable closure bundle.

This task exists because the final state of the project should be easy to evaluate later without reconstructing the whole campaign from chat logs or scattered issue comments.","design":"# Design
1. Collect the evidence matrix, release gates, acceptance artifacts, docs updates, and major closure comments into one bead-native bundle.
2. Record any remaining risks or explicitly deferred work so the release candidate is honest.
3. Define the release-candidate checklist that future maintainers can reuse.
4. Ensure the bundle points back to the beads that generated the evidence instead of duplicating everything.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A future maintainer can evaluate the finish-line campaign from this bundle without reopening the old markdown plan.
2. Remaining risks or deferrals are explicit.
3. The release-candidate checklist is concrete and reusable.
4. The bundle serves as the final closure reference for this whole program.
5. The bundle indexes the unit, integration, and E2E scripts, remote `rch` commands, artifact locations, diagnostic surfaces, and first-run or recovery pathways generated by the program instead of summarizing them vaguely.","notes":"This is the capstone bead. It should read like the final answer to whether the project is actually over the goal line.

Its bundle must index the canonical shared contract in `docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md` before it summarizes downstream evidence.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.624570Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T22:18:19.501815Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["closure","evidence","goal-line","release-readiness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:46.535721Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.624570Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:46.988053Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:47.443110Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:47.926843Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:48.382488Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:11.253093Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:13.693797Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","title":"Expose live health, leak, and backpressure diagnostics through doctor, status, and robot surfaces","description":"# Goal
Make long-haul swarm health visible to operators and other agents without requiring ad hoc tracing sessions or profiler archaeology.

This task exists because instrumentation only helps users once the important signals are surfaced through the product in a stable, interpretable way. A polished finish line needs truthful, actionable health diagnostics for day-2 operations, not just internal counters buried in logs.","design":"# Design
1. Surface the signals created by instrumentation and release-gate work through operator-facing commands and machine-facing endpoints.
2. Present both concise human summaries and structured machine-readable output so humans and agents share the same truth.
3. Include the counters and state that matter for long-haul swarm operation: pane, tab, or session counts, reconnect or shutdown anomalies, backpressure or queue pressure, leak-suspicion signals, and guidance on when to escalate.
4. Back the surface with comprehensive unit coverage and at least one logged incident-style E2E scenario.
5. Keep the semantics stable enough that playbooks, docs, and acceptance scenarios can depend on them.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Operators can inspect key long-haul health signals without attaching a profiler or reading raw logs.
2. Human and machine surfaces expose the same authoritative health state with clear field semantics.
3. Comprehensive unit tests cover aggregation, thresholding, serialization, and degraded-state handling for the new diagnostics surfaces.
4. At least one deterministic E2E or incident-rehearsal script exercises normal and degraded paths with detailed timestamped logs and stored artifacts.
5. Playbooks, docs, and operator acceptance scenarios are updated to use these surfaces as the standard diagnostic entry point.","notes":"This bead turns internal reliability work into something operators can actually use when a swarm starts to misbehave under long-haul load.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:15.364320Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:01:12.386236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:01:12.386032Z","close_reason":"Implemented HealthDiagnosticsData, HealthLeakRiskData, HealthGuidance types in robot_types.rs with from_health_snapshot() builder. Added Health variant to ApiSurface enum. 21 comprehensive unit tests covering all health levels (green/yellow/red/black), backpressure tiers, fleet pressure tiers, crash loops, leak risk fields, lock contention, guidance generation, serde roundtrip, and envelope roundtrip. All 51 robot_api_contracts + health diagnostics tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","e2e-tests","goal-line","operator","reliability","robot-mode","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.720102Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:08.319158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:15.364320Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","title":"Polish first-run setup, environment validation, and recovery entrypoints for operators","description":"# Goal
Make the project pleasant and trustworthy from the first real operator interaction: initial setup, environment validation, first successful control-plane run, and entry into recovery workflows.

This task exists because `ft-xbnl0.5.4` proves fresh-install and recovery scenarios, but the plan did not yet contain a dedicated implementation bead that improves those entrypoints themselves. A polished finish line should not merely detect that setup and recovery are rough; it should explicitly make them operator-friendly.","design":"# Design
1. Improve the first-run path so operators can get from clone or install to a validated, ready-to-use FrankenTerm workspace without reading source code or guessing about prerequisites.
2. Make environment validation and doctor output actionable, including missing dependency diagnosis, configuration bootstrap issues, restore-state problems, and recovery entrypoints.
3. Ensure the first-run and recovery surfaces point naturally into the new diagnostic, playbook, and acceptance tooling instead of acting like separate subsystems.
4. Back the flow with comprehensive unit coverage for setup or validation logic plus deterministic E2E scenarios that exercise clean-start, broken-environment, and recovery-entry cases with detailed logs and stored artifacts.
5. Keep the result honest: if some setup or recovery path is intentionally manual, document it clearly and make the product surface say so directly.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Operators can move from clean environment to validated first successful run using a documented and product-supported path rather than tribal knowledge.
2. Missing prerequisites, bad config, restore-state issues, and other common startup failures produce actionable doctor or status output with clear next steps.
3. Recovery entrypoints route operators toward the standard diagnostic and playbook surfaces rather than leaving them in ad hoc failure states.
4. Comprehensive unit tests cover setup-state detection, degraded-environment handling, and operator-facing error classification for the touched surfaces.
5. At least one deterministic E2E scenario exercises clean setup, one exercises a broken-environment or bad-config path, and one exercises recovery entry, all with detailed timestamped logging, artifact capture, and exact verification commands recorded.","notes":"This bead makes fresh-install and recovery usability an explicit implementation concern rather than something only checked at the very end by acceptance scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.049428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T17:15:33.968982Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T17:15:33.968636Z","close_reason":"operator guidance landed; artifact docs/ft-xbnl0-5-7-completion-evidence.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","e2e-tests","goal-line","operator","recovery","setup","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:12.565115Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.049428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:11.711759Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:12.253037Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -3,6 +3,11 @@
 This playbook is a pragmatic guide for keeping ft healthy during day-to-day use.
 It focuses on fast diagnosis, safe remediation, and actionable artifacts.
 
+Operator reality check:
+- `ft doctor`, `ft status --health`, `ft triage`, crash bundles, search, and the local evidence surfaces are native `ft` operator paths.
+- Live pane/session operations still rely on the current WezTerm-backed mux interop boundary.
+- Support and verification claims in this guide are anchored by `docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md`, `docs/ft-xbnl0-4-6-completion-evidence.md`, and `docs/ft-xbnl0-5-7-completion-evidence.md`.
+
 ## Quick start
 
 ```bash
@@ -19,6 +24,28 @@ Related guides:
 - For release posture and per-tier fallback defaults, use `docs/resize-user-facing-release-tuning-guidance-wa-1u90p.8.5.md`.
 - For exact knob meanings and safe starting ranges, use `docs/tuning-reference.md`.
 - For GUI-specific fleet operation, use `docs/frankenterm-gui-user-guide.md`.
+- For the supported-path honesty sweep, use `docs/ft-xbnl0-3-6-supported-path-truth-sweep.md`.
+
+---
+
+## First Run / Bootstrap
+
+Use this path on a fresh host or after any environment drift.
+
+```bash
+ft doctor
+ft doctor --json
+ft status --health
+wezterm cli list
+ft watch --foreground
+ft robot state
+```
+
+Interpretation:
+- `ft doctor` / `ft status --health` validate the local runtime, workspace, database, and operator guidance surfaces immediately.
+- `wezterm cli list` validates the current live mux interop boundary for pane/session operations.
+- `ft watch --foreground` is the fastest way to force first-run bootstrap of `.ft/`, logs, and the database while keeping diagnostics visible.
+- `ft robot state` confirms that the watcher plus live pane discovery path agree on what is observable.
 
 ---
